hadith_entries.text:
341. Narrated by Jaber ibn Abdullah: 'The Messenger of
Allah used to teach us Salat Al-Istikhara (the Prayer for
Guidance) in all affairs, as he used to teach us the Sora s of the
Koran. He said: "If any of you intended to do any affair, let him
perform two Raka'as other than the obligatory prayer, then he
says: 'O Allah! I seek your counsel by Your knowledge, and by
your power I seek strength, and ask You of Your abundant
bounty. For You are Able and I am not. And You are All -
Knowing and I am not. And You are the One Knowing the
unseen. O Allah! If You know that this affair is good for me in
my religion, my livelihood, and the final end of mine, or he said
in the present and the deferred affairs of mine, so facilitate it for
me. And if You know that this affair is evil for me in my
religion, my livelihood, and the final end of mine, or he said in
the present and the deferred affairs of mine, detract it from me
and detract me from it and decree good for me wherever it
might be and let me be content therewith.' (At-Termizi)
